Bring to the stage Mr. Brasso Brasso! Alright, I'm gonna stop there for the very beginning. My hands are sweating reliving the moment. We had a really unique chance to speak at Grant Cardone's 10X event and I was like, I want to do something that I've never done before and I was like, I want to net a million dollars in sales and it obviously worked really, really well. The difference is that I'm actually a really, really bad salesperson and I'm really, really good at selling to the masses. I'm just one of the few who still do it. It's a dead art. It's a skill that people understand. When you understand it though, it's the most powerful thing in the world. I don't care how good of a salesperson you are selling one-on-one, there's none of you guys that can do $3 million dollars in 90 minutes because you can't talk to enough people. The biggest thing you can do during your time with them on stage is to figure out what is keeping them back. You are breaking false beliefs and you do that through story. What's the story they're telling themselves and what story do I have that trumps their story? If I do it right, all these false beliefs disappear. Boom, and the domino falls down. Like from slide number two, you guys will notice, I'm going future-based collage, I'm talking about the four-minute mile. I'm talking about the future where we're going and people are buying that immediately. That's the real reason why they want the thing too. No one cares about making a million bucks. Nobody does. Why do they do it? They want a feeling. Now if you leave the backstory before you have connection, you're going to be in trouble with it. If you've watched a movie and the end of it, people died and you're like, huh, it's because they didn't spend a time in the backstory. Provement offer means you're going to help people improve. They're going to become better, faster, stronger, smarter. Most of you guys are selling the proven offer right now. That's why you're struggling. If you can't be the lowest price leader in town, there is zero strategic advantage about being the second lowest price leader. So you can't be the lowest price leader, be the most expensive because there's a huge strategic advantage of being the most expensive. Do not do a race to the bottom. I wanted to give you guys a framework. So when you leave here today, you've got something to start running with. Those two pieces are enough to make most of you guys millionaires. The whole goal of this is to basically knock down the big domino. What's the one thing I need them to believe? So I spent about 15 minutes right here on this part. It gets me an hour and my sack and my clothes is about 30 minutes. So that's the timeline. It's just weird thing with commitment. When people pay, they pay attention. You're going to talk about success. I'm going to be pointing to the back of the room where the tables are at. Every time I talk about failure, I'm going to point to the exit door. It's pushing people subconsciously to where I need them to go to be successful. Now you saw how a sneak attack out the sales letter order forms and the pens to everybody. Testimony is one of the most magical things we have. Make sure they don't miss that. I can reinforce it, try out clothes, push it down, origin story, price, marinade, secret one, secret two, secret three, vehicle, internal, external matches. They offer stack slide, moving them through stack slide, if all statements and then close it from there. If that's really your goal, that's really what you're looking for. And if I need you to believe that this is the only way to do it, then you have to give me money. Does that make sense? Every one of these lights represents an entrepreneur or salesperson. And for me, the only people in the world that are going to change this world for the better are entrepreneurs and salespeople. If I can help you to get your message, your product, your service out to more people, that's how the world is going to change. And that's what we're going to be doing today. I didn't want to be a stage presenter. I didn't want to sell stuff from stage. I didn't want to talk to people. I have super introverts still having... What I did want is the same thing I think all you guys want. This. We have customers. We care about them. We know we have a product or service that gives them a result and it lights us up and give them that result, right? And so for me, it's like I had to learn the skills and I understand it. Was it worth it? Yeah. There's not much more I can do with money. It doesn't really matter to me. The income stops firing up pretty quick, but the impact lasts forever.
